utterances: Anyway, I wanted to know if there was an underground, post-apocalyptic U.S.
Capitol - a secret bunker for Congress somewhere.
Well, I don't want to say.
But for 30 years - between 1961 and 1992 - that secret U.S.
Capitol building was behind this giant, reinforced-steel door.
Welcome to Capitol Hill the day after - except this isn't Washington.
It's a giant, concrete box nestled into a hillside in White Sulphur Springs, West Virginia.
The story of how it was kept secret for 30 years, and how it even got here, is stranger than any conspiracy theory.
I figured it out by first writing out all the letters that I thought would work either right-side-up or lying down on their side.
And then I just thought of words that I could make.
And it was strange because coming up won and one for me took about five or 10 minutes but then I sat on it for two days thinking am I really supposed to be making a W in an E?
Have you playing the puzzle a long time?
Yeah, about a decade, I guess.
Not from the postcards days but I think Will's probably wasted about, collectively, maybe 10 days of my life.
I get 10 minutes of his life right now.
Ben, you live in Dobbs Ferry, New York.
What is the best thing about Dobbs Ferry?
Dobbs Ferry is right on the Hudson River.
It's about maybe 10 miles north of New York City.
So, I would say I like living here because you can be in New York City in a half hour by train, but it really feels and works like a small Hudson River Valley town.
It doesn't feel like a suburb.
It feels like a little town.
Well, we'll see if all of your neighbors are listening to you play the puzzle.
Speaking of neighbors - I think Will is not too far.
I've never met him but I believe he doesn't live all that far down the road.
Yeah, also in New York state.
Up the road actually - about 15 minutes is all.
Will, hit us up with the puzzle?
Every answer is a familiar two-word phrase or name with the initials J-R.
For example, if I said a bread for a pastrami sandwich, maybe, you would say Jewish rye.
Number one: what you might take in a stolen car.
What a married couple files with the IRS.
Distilled alcoholic beverage from the Caribbean.
Voluptuous femme fatale in a 1988 animated film.
Where a group of 12 men and women deliberate after a trial.
Chief justice of the Supreme Court.
Baseball great with the retired number 42.
Elvis Presley hit that was number one for seven weeks.
